title: Audit Ops
sources: README.md: Operational standards and Private Era philosophy
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District D
database.sql: PostgreSQL schema for memo validation and role tracking
forom-config.ts: Mapping for Category D (Opérations) and Action 5 (Accompli)
App.tsx: Quest reward (2 PX) and completion logic implementation
resume: The quest 'Audit Ops' (D5) represents a triumphant milestone within the Opérations district, marking the first comprehensive validation of the community's internal processes at the 'Accompli' coordinate (5) on the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), an operational audit is not merely a bureaucratic check; it is a vital ritual of self-correction required to achieve the mission of 'Saving human communities and long-term memory.' Think of it as ensuring the digital plumbing is leak-proof before we fill the reservoir with ten years of human history. This quest signifies that the initial workflows (D0), feedback loops (D1), and technical setups (D3) have been rigorously tested and deemed functional enough to support the node’s long-term survival. Technically, this audit involves a systematic review of the 'memos' table in the local PostgreSQL database, ensuring that all entries within the Opérations district meet the high-quality standards necessary for the foundational archive. The Forom stack, utilizing React 19 and Vite 7, provides the 9 Super-Moderators with a high-performance 5x5 spatial viewport to verify that the operational 'Deep Lore' is correctly indexed and accessible. Hosting this audit locally on an NVIDIA Jetson ensures that the community’s internal mechanics remain private and sovereign, honoring the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy of total digital autonomy. Economically, successfully passing the D5 audit rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, contributing to the healthy redistribution of purchasing power within the node. This internal economy, fueled by the initial 500 PX treasury of the Super-Mods, ensures that the 1209-user population is incentivized to maintain high operational standards. The 'Guardian' role (Red) and 'Social' role (Blue) work in tandem here—one ensuring procedural stability and the other managing the transparency of the audit results. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, D5 is the ultimate guarantee of continuity; it confirms that the operational 'baton' is ready to be passed to the next semester of students without friction. Once archived, this audit memo becomes a crucial part of the V1 dataset that ROM, the local RAG engine, will ingest in Phase 3. ROM will use this status to benchmark future performance and provide automated suggestions for efficiency. By canonizing the audit at D5, the community transforms a temporary check-up into a permanent asset. Operational excellence: because even a sovereign digital fortress needs to know if the door hinges are squeaky. The system is validated. Yes.
summary: This quest marks the formal validation of the community's operational workflows at coordinate D5, ensuring the node is efficient and ready for the 100-memo goal.
